postmanEvidence: Postman Desktop 11.71.7 packages postman-sandbox@6.2.2 for script execution.
The same postman-sandbox probe records direct eval, indirect eval, Function, Buffer, host-like globals, and constructor escape behavior.

commands: npm install postman-sandbox@6.2.2 postman-collection@5.2.0 in an isolated temporary directory
Run the dynamic host-global probe through postman-sandbox@6.2.2 createContext/execute
Run the same Newman-compatible probe through newman@6.2.2 and PostMeter via npm run postman:parity:diff -- --require-newman --download-newman

commonDynamicCodeProfile.indirectEval: supported inside the script sandbox global scope
commonDynamicCodeProfile.Function: supported inside the script sandbox global scope
commonDynamicCodeProfile.constructorConstructorEscape: does not expose process or host Node handles

commonHostLikeGlobalProfile.Buffer: available as a top-level script binding, absent from Function('return this')() and Function-created code

desktopOnlyProfile.File: postman-sandbox@6.2.2 exposes File as a top-level script binding; newman@6.2.2 does not. PostMeter keeps the safe File facade for Desktop import compatibility and keeps it out of Function-created global code.

postmeterDecision: Match the common Postman sandbox profile for default imports: direct and indirect eval are enabled only inside the isolated VM, Function remains enabled but sees no host process/fetch/Buffer, top-level Buffer uses PostMeter's hardened buffer facade, raw fetch/XHR/WebSocket/WebAssembly/process/globalThis/browser globals remain absent, and Desktop's top-level File binding remains a safe facade.
notes: This audit clears the dynamic-code and host-like globals claim blocker while preserving the parent-brokered network and package policies required by the supported import profile.
